preamble: Alveolarization of the voiceless dental fricative [θ] to the voiceless alveolar fricative [s] is a frequent phonetic shift in both historical developments and non-native speech patterns, where the tongue position shifts slightly backward to the alveolar ridge.

examples.note: Classical Arabic /θ/ has shifted to [s] in many urban dialects, particularly in loanwords.
Common substitution by speakers whose native languages lack dental fricatives but have a robust sibilant [s].
